name: cursor-copilot-bridge
description: Use Cursor CLI models in GitHub Copilot Chat — no proxy, no server, just works.

cursorBridge.apiKey.markdownDescription:
Cursor API key for authentication. Leave empty to use CLI's built-in auth (requires `agent login` first).

Get your key: [cursor.com/settings](https://cursor.com/settings)

cursorBridge.agentPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the Cursor CLI executable.

**Important: On Windows, use the full path ending with `agent.cmd`**, e.g.:
- `C:\Users\you\.cursor\cli\agent.cmd`
- `C:\CursorToolkit\bin\agent.cmd`

The default value `agent` only works if the CLI directory is in your system PATH. If you're unsure, run `where agent` in a terminal to find the full path, then paste it here.

**macOS/Linux**: use the full path to the `agent` binary, or keep default if it's in PATH.

Install Cursor CLI:
- **Windows**: `irm 'https://cursor.com/install?win32=true' | iex`
- **macOS/Linux**: `curl -fsSL https://cursor.com/install | bash`

After install, run `agent login` to authenticate.

cursorBridge.defaultModel.markdownDescription:
Default model ID, e.g. `opus-4.6-thinking`, `sonnet-4.6`, `gpt-4o`.

Leave empty to expose all available models in the Copilot model picker.

cursorBridge.force.markdownDescription: Auto-approve all tool invocations without prompting (maps to `--force` / `--yolo`). When disabled, each tool call will wait for confirmation.

cursorBridge.approveMcps.markdownDescription: Automatically approve all MCP servers (maps to `--approve-mcps`).

enumDescriptions: Force sandbox on (commands run in an isolated environment)
Force sandbox off (commands run directly on the system)
cursorBridge.sandbox.markdownDescription: Override sandbox mode for shell commands. Sandbox prevents the AI from executing dangerous system commands.

cursorBridge.customHeaders.markdownDescription:
Custom HTTP headers to add to agent requests, format: `Name: Value`.

Example: `Authorization: Bearer xxx`

cursorBridge.sessionTtlMinutes.markdownDescription: Session idle timeout in minutes. Expired sessions are cleaned up automatically; the next message will start a new session.

cursorBridge.maxHistoryTurns.markdownDescription: Max conversation turns to carry over when starting a new CLI session. Higher values give more context but cost more tokens.

cursorBridge.timeoutSeconds.markdownDescription: Max time in seconds to wait for a CLI response. Complex tasks may need more time. Default is 300s (5 min).

cursorBridge.maxMode.markdownDescription:
Enable Cursor **Max Mode** for all requests. Max mode extends context window size and tool-call limits.

When enabled, the bridge writes `maxMode: true` to `cli-config.json` before each request. The CLI resets it after use, so there is no permanent side-effect.

cursorBridge.verbose.markdownDescription: Enable verbose logging in the `Output → Cursor Bridge` panel. Recommended when troubleshooting issues.
